steps.title: Determine what “constantly” means in context
steps.content:
The passage shows Alexander avoiding clubs and dining alone at his hotel — every detail signals that all his time goes to work with no social breaks. “Constantly” here means working without interruption, not describing speed or emotion. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — ‘Nonstop’: without stopping or pausing — matches the uninterrupted, round-the-clock work implied by his avoidance of all social outlets. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Quickly’: at a fast pace — describes speed, not duration; one can work quickly for a short time, missing the relentless continuity the passage conveys. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Fearfully’: with anxiety or dread — describes an emotional state; nothing in the passage suggests Alexander is afraid while working. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Enough’: to a sufficient degree — implies he worked adequately, not without interruption; it fails to capture the unrelenting nature his isolation implies. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only “Nonstop” conveys continuous, uninterrupted work, matching the context of Alexander avoiding all social activity. That matches choice A — A is correct.
